title: High-Priced Sickle Cell Gene Therapies Threaten to Exacerbate US Health Disparities and Establish New Pricing Precedents for Molecular Medicine.
author_string: Tessema, Frazer A, Sarpatwari, Ameet, Rand, Leah Z, Kesselheim, Aaron S
journal_full: The Journal of law, medicine & ethics : a journal of the American Society of Law, Medicine & Ethics
abstract: Gene therapies to treat sickle cell disease are in development and are expected to have high costs. The large eligible population size - by far, the largest for a gene therapy - poses daunting budget challenges and threatens to exacerbate health disparities for Black patients, who make up the vast majority of American sickle cell patients.
